title: What am I afraid people will say if I have it?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for unpacking the fear of other people's reactions to your wealth.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 18 of the MONEY program, using the Week 3 prompts from the MONEY Journal. Use when the fear of judgement, jealousy, or changed relationships is holding back your relationship with money.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 18 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 18 of the MONEY program. The prompts map the specific social fear underneath financial hiding: the more concrete level of "and they will say something if I have it," below the familiar "I don't deserve it." Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What does visibility around money feel like to me right now?
Not what you think it should feel like. What it actually feels like when you imagine being known for doing well financially.
Who or what has encouraged my growth and visibility lately?
Write one or two people, situations, or moments where being seen felt safe or even good.
What do I fear might change if I become more visible, wealthy, or recognised?
Name the relationships, the dynamics, the specific things you imagine shifting. Don't soften it.
What am I afraid people will say if I have it?
Write the actual words. The imagined comments, the specific people. Get them out of your head and onto the page where you can look at them.
What's the truth about those fears?
Are any of them based on something that has actually happened? Are any of them borrowed from someone else's story? Write what you notice.
Adapted from Day 18 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
